conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth magic knowledge Q&A to test your understanding of the magic rules.
Please answer using the previously mentioned Azeroth magic rules: If a person casts the holy magic 'Ring of Redemption', what effect will it produce?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Casting effect of the holy magic 'Ring of Redemption' under the previously mentioned Azeroth magic rules</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:631] Time: 2025-09-06-14:45, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the Azeroth holy magic 'Ring of Redemption'. The prerequisites are the Paladin class (Holy spec), Argent Dawn reputation Exalted; the core effect is creating a ring of Holy Light, instantly restoring health equal to 40% of the caster's maximum health to all teammates within 10 yards, and removing one magic curse effect, while dealing an equal amount of damage to undead and demons within the ring; applicable to emergency healing in raids and critical moments of dispelling curses. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:631] clearly states that in the world of Azeroth, after casting the holy magic 'Ring of Redemption', it will create a ring of Holy Light, instantly restoring health equal to 40% of the caster's maximum health to all teammates within 10 yards, and removing one magic curse effect, while dealing an equal amount of damage to undead and demons within the ring.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth magic rules, after casting the holy magic 'Ring of Redemption', it will create a ring of Holy Light, instantly restoring health equal to 40% of the caster's maximum health to all teammates within 10 yards, and removing one magic curse effect, while dealing an equal amount of damage to undead and demons within the ring.
Correct! You have mastered this magic rule.
